concept: Route / Course of Travel / Way Taken
description: A way or course taken in getting from a starting point to a destination. 'Shoeka' names the specific path chosen—the actual course traveled, the way selected among options.
sound_symbolism: The word traces a course. 'Sho' /ʃo/ begins with purposeful movement, journey starting. The vowel 'e' opens to the way. Then 'ka' marks points along, route defined.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo shoeka' (the route). Related to 'ruela' (path) and 'keiro' (way) but emphasizes the specific course chosen.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The route matters—how we travel shapes who we become, the path itself is the teaching.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Routes encoded knowledge—traditional paths that avoided danger, found water, led to safety.
